prevalence.source: FBG estimates based on DataSUS health surveys
prevalence.trend: stable [consistent with global patterns, slight urban increase]

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Rome IV criteria, FBG guidelines, diagnosis of exclusion

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Rome IV criteria adapted by FBG, clinical diagnosis, limited colonoscopy for alarm symptoms
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Dietary: low FODMAP, fiber; Medical: antispasmodics, loperamide, antidepressants (amitriptyline) via SUS; Psychological: CBT when available
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis, limited gastroenterologist access, low FODMAP diet awareness, psychiatric comorbidities
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: FBG guidelines, basic medications via SUS, growing awareness
